_cite.reason: 4c: relabel unlabeled workspace-analysis to labeled analysis (o4 §15); every binding sentence judged WORKSPACE_ANALYSIS

prior.content_md:
| # | Title | Issuing Body | Type | Date | URL |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Rules on Ship Carbon Intensity and Rating System Enter into Force | IMO | Intergovernmental body press briefing (Tier 3) | 1 November 2022 | https://www.imo.org/en/MediaCentre/PressBriefings/pages/CII-and-EEXI-entry-into-force.aspx |
| 2 | Resolution MEPC.338(76) — 2021 Guidelines on the Operational Carbon Intensity Reduction Factors Relative to Reference Lines (CII Reduction Factors Guidelines, G3) | IMO | Intergovernmental body resolution (Tier 3) | 2021 | https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/KnowledgeCentre/IndexofIMOResolutions/MEPCDocuments/MEPC.338(76).pdf |
| 3 | Resolution MEPC.377(80) — 2023 IMO Strategy on Reduction of GHG Emissions from Ships | IMO | Intergovernmental body resolution (Tier 3) | 7 July 2023 | https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/KnowledgeCentre/IndexofIMOResolutions/MEPCDocuments/MEPC.377(80).pdf |
| 4 | 2023 IMO Strategy on Reduction of GHG Emissions from Ships (overview page) | IMO | Intergovernmental body overview (Tier 3) | 2023 | https://www.imo.org/en/ourwork/environment/pages/2023-imo-strategy-on-reduction-of-ghg-emissions-from-ships.aspx |
| 5 | EEXI and CII — Ship Carbon Intensity and Rating System (FAQ) | IMO | Intergovernmental body FAQ (Tier 3) | Ongoing | https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/hottopics/pages/eexi-cii-faq.aspx |
| 6 | International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships (MARPOL) | IMO | Binding convention overview (Tier 3) | Ongoing | https://www.imo.org/en/about/conventions/pages/international-convention-for-the-prevention-of-pollution-from-ships-(marpol).aspx |

---

*No content for the Exemptions and Edge Cases section as of this brief: The available source material does not identify any CII exemptions applicable to the cargo verticals or transport configurations in the workspace profile that are distinct from the general 5,000 GT threshold and the domestic-voyage carve-out.*

*No content for the Adjacent Industry Research and Alternatives section as of this brief: No sourced carrier-specific or route-specific research comparing CII mitigation strategies (slow steaming vs. fuel substitution vs. retrofitting) was available in the source pool provided. This is a research gap that should be populated from ICCT, Transport & Environment, or carrier sustainability reports.*

*No content for the Product-Specific Compliance Status section: The workspace does not sell products in scope for CII. CII applies to vessel operators, not to cargo types or freight forwarder products.*

---
